title: Entropy
subtitle: Guess the next word of the text — Kolmogorov's experiment, 1965
intro:
You are shown an excerpt from a literary text. The final word is hidden. Guess it — in as few attempts as possible.

In 1965, Kolmogorov described experiments in which subjects guessed the continuation of literary passages. The results were used to compute an upper bound on the conditional entropy of natural language. The fewer guesses you need, the lower your implied entropy.

Your score is the mean entropy in bits per word. Lower is better. A perfect reader who always guesses on the first try compresses Austen to zero bits.

One attempt at a time. Five attempts per word. Enter the exact word form as it appears in the text — no lemmatisation.

ui.lb_min_words: At least 20 words required to appear on the leaderboard.
ui.no_stats: No data yet — play at least one word.
